title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for request lines, methods, target forms, versions, and routing inputs and requires the smallest strict strict request-line and request-target parser with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for strict request-line and request-target parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.
Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Line Delimiters
steps.instruction:
Order the Request Line Delimiters evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Line Delimiters.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Request Line Delimiters assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Line Delimiters, keep Request Line Delimiters connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A quiz protocol team handles case 1365 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Request Line Delimiters has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
